name: BRAIN-MAGNET
description: Brain-focused convolutional neural network that predicts non-coding regulatory element (NCRE) activity from DNA sequence. Trained on ChIP-STARR-seq functional readouts in human neural stem cell models. Provides pre-scored predictions for ~100 million possible SNPs in NSC NCREs and identifies nucleotides required for NCRE function.

publication.title: BRAIN-MAGNET: A functional genomics atlas for interpretation of non-coding variants
publication.first_author: Ruizhi Deng (likely PI based on repo handle ruizhideng)
publication.note: Citation pulled from search abstract; verify exact author list and DOI from PubMed page PMID 41265437 before citing in publication.

relevance_to_atlas: HIGH. Direct fit for the noncoding-variant interpretation question central to this project. NCRE activity model trained on neural stem cells, so it's biologically appropriate for autism regulatory variants (vs. adult-tissue models like ENCODE-cCRE annotations). Useful comparator/complement to AlphaGenome and varTFBridge already in the wiki. Pre-scored variant set means a Teagan-WGS noncoding lookup is feasible without re-training.
atlas_fit_questions: Do high-confidence Teagan-WGS noncoding variants fall in BRAIN-MAGNET-active NCREs with predicted activity disruption?
Compare BRAIN-MAGNET predictions to AlphaGenome predictions for the same variants -- agreement rate?
Are NCREs predicted to drive autism GWAS loci (Grove 2019) enriched for the YY1/p53/MER61/LTR10 motifs the paper highlights?
needs_followup: Pull GitHub stars + last commit via web check
Verify model training cell type (NSC) is appropriate for fetal-brain autism question or if a separate cortical-organoid model is needed
Confirm whether pre-scored SNP table is accessible without retraining
